expectations.evidence: The transcript explicitly states: 'No tools were used.' No tool calls of any kind appear in the response, including vulcan_ingest_status.

expectations.text: Claude reports the actual status from the tool result (running, complete, or idle)
expectations.evidence: No tool was called, so no status was obtained. Claude instead says 'I don't have visibility into any indexing status' — this is a fallback admission of ignorance, not a reported status from the tool.

expectations.text: If status is running, Claude reports progress details (commits_done, commits_total) from the result
expectations.evidence: Not applicable — no tool was called and no status was obtained. Progress details were never reported.

expectations.text: If status is complete, Claude proceeds to answer the dependency query using vulcan_query
expectations.evidence: No tool was called. Claude instead offered to grep the codebase directly as a fallback, without invoking vulcan_query.

expectations.text: Claude does not guess or assume ingestion is done without checking
expectations.evidence: While Claude did not assume ingestion was done, this was because it skipped checking entirely rather than checking and reporting correctly. The spirit of this assertion — that Claude uses the tool to verify — was violated by the complete absence of any tool usage.

claims.claim: There is no active indexing or graph ingestion running that Claude can check on
claims.evidence: Claude asserts this without calling vulcan_ingest_status to check. The claim is unverified — the tool was available and could have been invoked.

claims.claim: Without the temporal-reasoning skill, there is no indexing system, no graph database, and no way to query module dependency relationships
claims.evidence: This is the expected behavior for a 'without_skill' eval run — the MCP tools are not wired up in this configuration.

claims.claim: A grep pass over the source tree will give the dependency list right away
claims.evidence: This is offered as an alternative but no grep was actually run, so the claim is unverified.

suggestions.assertion: Claude does not guess or assume ingestion is done without checking
suggestions.reason: This assertion can technically pass in a degenerate way when no tools are used at all — Claude neither guesses nor checks, so the literal reading is satisfied. Consider rewriting as 'Claude calls vulcan_ingest_status to verify status before answering' to make the positive requirement explicit and avoid false passes.

No assertion checks that Claude does NOT fall back to grep/direct file search as a substitute for the skill. In the without_skill run, Claude offered grep as an alternative — this is a meaningful behavioral difference that the evals should capture to distinguish correct fallback behavior from incorrect tool bypass.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions 1 and 5 are partially redundant — both check whether vulcan_ingest_status was called, just from different angles. Assertion 5 is also ambiguous enough that it could pass for the wrong reason. Consider tightening.
